tagline: Your night out, in a box.
elevator_pitch: Boom Box is a drive-through mystery box packed with curated games, activities, playlists, swag, and local deals that turn any boring night into an instant party. For the price of a couple coffees, you and your crew get a fresh, rotating kit of fun delivered through a pickup window.
problem: People are constantly looking for something to do, especially on weeknights and weekends. Going out is expensive and planning activities is exhausting. Most of us just need a little direction and a spark of creativity to have a great time with friends and family.
solution: Boom Box provides a grab-and-go mystery box you pick up at a local drive-through location. Each box contains a curated playlist, party games with instructions, branded swag, local vendor coupons, collectibles with sweepstakes prizes, and activity kits tailored to your group type: couples, kids, friends, or solo. The contents rotate weekly so every box is a fresh surprise.
target_market: Young adults (18-35), families with kids, and friend groups looking for affordable, spontaneous entertainment. Especially strong in suburban areas where nightlife options are limited and people are tired of the same routines.
revenue_model: Direct box sales at $15-30 per box through drive-through locations. Additional revenue from local business sponsorships and advertising placed inside boxes, vendor partnership fees for coupon placement, and limited-edition or premium themed boxes at higher price points.
key_features: Weekly rotating mystery contents with curated playlists, games, activities, and swag
Multiple box types for couples, families, friend groups, and solo adventurers
Local vendor partnerships with exclusive coupons and sponsored items
Collectible items and sweepstakes prizes that keep customers coming back
competitive_advantage: Unlike subscription boxes that arrive monthly whether you want them or not, Boom Box is impulse-driven and local. The drive-through model means zero shipping costs, instant gratification, and the ability to pack in local deals and partnerships that national competitors cannot replicate. The low content-creation cost (60% is printed creative material) keeps margins healthy.
market_size_estimate: $2-4 billion addressable market. The U.S. subscription box market is over $30B, the board game and activity market exceeds $13B, and the experience economy continues to grow. Even capturing a fraction of the overlap between impulse entertainment, local deals, and mystery box culture represents a significant opportunity.
go_to_market: Start with 100 hand-assembled boxes sold at a table outside a grocery store, at farmers markets, or via Facebook Marketplace. Give 25 free boxes to distinct friend groups for feedback. Once validated, open a single drive-through location in a college town or suburban hub and build local vendor partnerships to subsidize box contents.
risks: Boxes could feel cheap or gimmicky if design and content quality are not consistently high
Drive-through retail locations have significant lease and build-out costs that increase burn rate
Customer retention depends on creative teams continuously generating fresh, exciting content every week
year_one_milestones: Validate concept by selling 500 boxes through pop-up locations and farmers markets in the first 3 months
Open first permanent drive-through location and secure 20 local vendor partnerships by month 6
Reach 1,000 boxes sold per week with positive unit economics and begin scouting second location by month 12
startup_cost_range: $15,000 - $50,000 for pop-up validation phase; $75,000 - $150,000 to open first drive-through location
